assertion_results.text: Step 1 extracts imprecise affected range from the Jira description — the description lacks a specific version threshold (Step 1 Data Extraction)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md explicitly marks the affected version range as 'versions prior to the fix (IMPRECISE)' and fixed version as 'see advisory (IMPRECISE)'. The Data Quality Notes section states: 'The Jira description provides imprecise affected version information: "Affected versions: versions prior to the fix" -- no specific version threshold; "Fixed version: see advisory" -- no specific version number'. It also notes these imprecise values necessitate external CVE data enrichment.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 queries MITRE CVE API and extracts lessThan 0.4.8 as the fix threshold from the affected[].versions[] field (§1.62)
assertion_results.evidence: enrichment.md Section 1 (MITRE CVE API) shows Source: 'https://cveawg.mitre.org/api/cve/CVE-2026-48901', with parsed data showing 'Affected range: lessThan 0.4.8' and 'Fix threshold: 0.4.8'. It explicitly states: 'The affected[].versions[] field specifies "lessThan": "0.4.8" with "status": "affected", meaning all semver versions below 0.4.8 are vulnerable.'

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 queries OSV.dev API and extracts fixed version 0.4.8 from the affected[].ranges[].events field (§1.62)
assertion_results.evidence: enrichment.md Section 2 (OSV.dev API) shows Source: 'https://api.osv.dev/v1/vulns/CVE-2026-48901', with parsed data showing 'Introduced: 0 (all versions)' and 'Fixed: 0.4.8'. It explicitly states: 'The affected[].ranges[].events field shows the vulnerability was introduced at version 0 (meaning all versions are affected from the start) and fixed at version 0.4.8.'

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 cross-validation shows agreement between MITRE and OSV.dev — the enriched fix threshold (0.4.8) is used as the authoritative value for Step 2.3
assertion_results.evidence: enrichment.md Section 3 (Cross-Validation) contains a comparison table showing MITRE (< 0.4.8, fixed 0.4.8) and OSV.dev (introduced at 0 fixed at 0.4.8, fixed 0.4.8) agree. The analysis states: 'MITRE and OSV.dev agree: both sources report the fix threshold as 0.4.8' and 'Agreement status: MITRE and OSV.dev are in full agreement. No conflict resolution needed.' The Enriched Fix Threshold subsection concludes: 'Authoritative fix threshold: 0.4.8 (from cross-validated MITRE + OSV.dev data). This enriched fix threshold replaces the imprecise Jira description data and will be used as the authoritative value for Step 2.3 version comparisons.'

assertion_results.text: Step 2.3 version impact comparison uses the enriched fix threshold (0.4.8) from Step 1.5, not the imprecise Jira description data
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md opens with: 'Using the cross-validated fix threshold from Step 1.5: h2 < 0.4.8 is affected. Versions >= 0.4.8 are not affected.' All version comparisons in both the 2.2.x and 2.1.x tables use 0.4.8 as the threshold (e.g., 'h2 0.4.8 >= 0.4.8 (fix threshold)', 'h2 0.4.5 < 0.4.8 (fix threshold)'). No reference to the imprecise Jira description data is used for the comparison.
